title: Parmesan Cheese Crisps Laced with Zucchini & Carrots
excerpt: Parmesan Cheese Crisps Laced with Zucchini & Carrots. A home-cook's recipe from Rebecca's collection, kept in the family rotation.
sourceNotes: From Rebecca's personal cookbook, a recipe she's made many times in her own kitchen.

content.text: Parmesan Cheese Crisps Laced with Zucchini & Carrots. A British home-cook's dish, the kind of recipe families adjust across generations. Adjustable for what you need.
A weeknight-friendly recipe: most of the work is the prep, and the cooking takes care of itself.
What you need

attrs.body: Parchment paper or silicone baking mat mat.
attrs.title: Also

content.text: Method
Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
Using a paper towel, remove excess moisture from shredded zucchini and carrots.
Combine Parmesan cheese and shredded vegetables.
Place Tablespoon size drops of the cheese and vegetable mixture on a baking sheet topped with parchment paper or a silicone baking mat mat.
Gently flatten mixture with the back of a spoon, if necessary, and move in any loose shreds.
Bake for about 8 minutes or until the cheese crisps are sizzling and the edges are starting to brown.
Allow crisps to rest on the baking sheet for about 5 minutes and then transfer them to a serving plate with a spatula. Crisps will continue to harden.
There are just a few things to keep in mind when making these easy little chips:

Use fresh Parmesan cheese. Keep some of the grated cheese in longer shreds. I used the Sargento brand Parmesan cheese that you find in the refrigerated section at the grocery store. The combination of “sticks” and grated cheese is perfect.
Grate zucchini and carrots and then press out excess moisture with a paper towel.
Use twice as much cheese as grated vegetable. The Parmesan cheese is the glue that holds everything together.

Drop spoonfuls of the Parmesan cheese and vegetable mixture on a baking sheet covered with parchment paper (not aluminum foil) or a silicone baking mat mat. If necessary, gently flatten with your finger or the back of a spoon and scoot in any loose pieces.
Bake Parmesan Cheese Crisps in a 375 degree F oven for about 8 minutes. The crisps should be sizzling when you take them out and the edges should be starting to brown.
Allow the crisps to settle on the baking sheet for about 5 minutes and then transfer them to a serving plate using a spatula. Crisps will continue to firm up.
For my dairy-free friends, the recipe works with vegan shreds!

attrs.intro: A few of the more common issues, and what tends to fix them.

items.fix: Salt a pinch at a time, tasting. A squeeze of lemon or splash of vinegar at the end lifts most savoury dishes.
Pull off the heat a minute or two before you think it's done. Plate straight away, or transfer to a warmed serving dish.
items.cause: Carryover cooking, food keeps cooking on the hot pan after the heat goes off.
items.symptom: It's overcooked by the time it reaches the table

items.fix: Drop the heat and give it longer. A probe thermometer is the only reliable check for meat.
items.cause: Heat too high, browned the outside before the middle warmed through.

items.fix: Reheat covered with a splash of stock or water, on a low heat. Microwave on 60% power if using one, gentler than full blast.
items.cause: Reheated uncovered, or no liquid in the pan.

attrs.heading: If something goes wrong

content.text: Variations
Vegetarian swap: replace meat with mushrooms, lentils, or beans, adjust seasoning to compensate.
Spice version: a pinch of chilli flakes or cracked black pepper at the end shifts the dish without losing its character.
Make ahead, freezing, leftovers
Best fresh. Leftovers keep two days in the fridge; reheat covered with a splash of liquid.
Where this dish lives
A British home cook's dish. It sits among the recipes families remember without thinking about, the kind that gets adjusted across generations and never quite the same twice.
